channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Daryl Hannah: ¿Cómo puede ‘Love Story’ salirse con la suya?
item.description: En general, he optado por no responder a la cobertura que se hace sobre mí. Pero una serie de televisión que explota la tragedia incluye a un personaje que utiliza mi nombre y la presenta como yo.
item.dc:creator: Daryl Hannah
item.category: Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Live Updates: New Wave of Airstrikes Batters Tehran
item.description: The Israeli military said Saturday that it had launched more strikes on Iran’s capital. Some hit near the Mehrabad Airport. Footage shared on state media showed explosions and columns of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: En una ‘fiesta del té’ con científicos, este simio demostró su imaginación
item.description: En un estudio realizado durante un recreo, los investigadores descubrieron que nuestros parientes vivos más cercanos también tienen la capacidad de imaginar.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil

item.title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
item.description: For decades, enthusiasts rode the cresting Eisbach creek in the southern German city, some 200 miles from the nearest coast. But the wave has vanished, prompting arguments about how to restore it.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Laetitia Vançon

media:content.media:credit: Laetitia Vancon for The New York Times

item.title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
item.description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Maxim Shemetov/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s top leader, during an annual legislative meeting in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Globe-Trotting U.S. Citizen Landed in a Venezuelan Hell
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, March 7, 2026.

item.title: Trump Calls On Private Companies to Take On a Bigger Role in Cyber
item.description: In his National Cybersecurity Strategy, President Trump sought an expanded role for private firms in cyberwarfare. He did not take on China or Russia in the document.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump on Friday released the National Cybersecurity Strategy, which calls for private companies to play a more active role.

item.title: Appeals Court Upholds Protected Status for 350,000 Haitians
item.description: The D.C. Circuit ruled against the Trump administration, ensuring Haitians can remain in the United States and keep working while the underlying lawsuit proceeds.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

media:content.media:credit: Ivan Pierre Aguirre for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The long-term fate of Haitians with T.P.S. is still unclear because the Trump administration is likely to file an emergency appeal with the Supreme Court.

item.title: State Department Bypasses Congress to Send Israel More Than 20,000 Bombs
item.description: The sale had been under informal review in Congress, but the State Department declared an emergency because of the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Multiple explosions near Mehrabad airport amid US-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Friday as part of their joint war on Iran.

item.title: Iran’s Navy Is Weakened but U.S. Still Faces Challenges in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. strikes have heavily targeted Iran’s large ships and bases, but its Revolutionary Guard navy is a more nimble force.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Nicolas Economou/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Port of Bandar Abbas in the Strait of Hormuz in 2023.

item.title: Energy
item.description: Fritz Juhnke’s first Saturday puzzle for The New York Times requires some agility.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Scott Barbour/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Competitors hurtling down a steep slope in pursuit of a large wheel of Double Gloucester — first one down gets the cheese.

item.title: Trump Maintains That U.S. Has Ample Weapons as Iran War Depletes Stocks
item.description: After meeting with defense contractors at the White House, President Trump said the companies were all committed to increasing production.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Farah Stockman and Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said an increase in weapons production would come “as rapidly as possible” to “the highest levels of quantity.”

item.title: Justice Dept. Denounces Federal Judges in Fight Against Law Firms
item.description: The Trump administration had signaled earlier this week that it was ready to abandon four executive orders seeking to punish law firms, but abruptly reversed course the next day.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: Wilmer Cutler Pickering Hale and Dorr LLP

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department is accusing federal judges of undermining President Trump’s authority.

item.title: Vulnerable California Republican Darrell Issa Drops Re-election Bid
item.description: The decisions by Darrell Issa and Kevin Kiley, made newly vulnerable by redistricting, demonstrated the challenges Republicans face in a difficult midterm environment.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Darrell Issa has served for 12 terms in the House.

item.title: The Pentagon’s Attack on Anthropic Is Political
item.description: What happens when the A.I. tools helping to run the country stop sharing the government’s goals? The former Trump A.I. adviser Dean Ball joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to discuss the looming threat of institutional misalignment.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Former Presidents Speak at Jesse Jackson’s Memorial
item.description: Barack Obama, Joseph R. Biden Jr. and Bill Clinton were among the dozens of speakers at a public memorial for the late Rev. Jesse Jackson in Chicago on Friday. The event celebrated the civil rights leader’s commitment to public service and racial justice.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The casket of the Rev. Jesse Jackson arrived at the House of Hope for a service in Chicago on Friday morning.

item.title: Trump Echoes F.D.R. and Grant in Calling for ‘Unconditional Surrender’
item.description: President Trump demanded that Iran capitulate in the war with the United States, invoking a phrase made famous by statesmen and generals.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump declared on Friday that he would settle for nothing short of “unconditional surrender” by Iran.

item.title: The Government’s A.I. Alignment Problem
item.description: The Pentagon’s attack on Anthropic is a signal of government-sanctioned suppression, Trump’s former A.I. adviser Dean Ball argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Mamdani Defends Wife Amid Criticism of Her Support for Palestinian Cause
item.description: Rama Duwaji, Mr. Mamdani’s wife, had liked Instagram posts related to the Hamas-led attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Sally Goldenberg
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said his wife, Rama Duwaji, is a “private person” who does not have a responsibility to answer for her thoughts the way that he does.

item.title: Brandon Herrera’s YouTube Gives Democrats More Hope in West Texas Race
item.description: What had been a safe G.O.P. seat was looking more attainable for Democrats after Representative Tony Gonzales bowed out in favor of a hard-right candidate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Brenda BazáN/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Brandon Herrera had forced a run-off with the incumbent, the Republican Tony Gonzales, before Mr. Gonzales ended his re-election bid.

item.title: Russia Gets Silent Treatment in Paralympics Return
item.description: Russia’s flag was paraded at the opening ceremony of the Paralympics for the first time since 2014 prompting a boycott by several nations led by Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Josephine de La Bruyère

media:content.media:credit: Luca Bruno/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Two Russian athletes, center, and Russian officials participated in the opening ceremony of the Winter Paralympics in Verona, Italy.

item.title: México anuncia medidas de seguridad para el Mundial
item.description: Las autoridades anunciaron un despliegue masivo de fuerzas de seguridad de cara al evento deportivo más grande del mundo, luego de que la violencia reciente generara preocupaciones por la seguridad de millones de aficionados.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and David Shortell

media:content.media:credit: Raquel Cunha/Reuters
media:content.media:description: El Estadio Banorte, también conocido como Estadio Azteca, el recinto de Ciudad de México que albergará el partido inaugural de la Copa del Mundo de 2026.

item.title: Kristi Noem Survived Many Crises. Then She Crossed a Trump Red Line.
item.description: President Trump, who values loyalty, has at times tried to distance himself from his administration’s own actions when they become politically toxic.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kristi Noem testifying before the Senate Judiciary Committee on Tuesday, where her comments led the president to fire her later in the week.

item.title: How the War in Iran Is Disrupting the World’s Oil
item.description: Our business reporter Peter Eavis looks at the global implications of the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway to the south of Iran. He analyzes ship activity in the strait, comparing it before and after the United States and Israel initiated attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Sutton Raphael, Leila Medina, Stephanie Swart, Blacki Migliozzi, Christiaan Triebert, Keith Collins, Jacqueline Gu and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran War Poses Test for Justice Dept. After Firings Deplete National Security Ranks
item.description: Firings, resignations and diversions to the president’s priorities have left elite counterterrorism and counterintelligence units stretched thin, current and former officials say.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Glenn Thrush
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Directives from the Trump administration have led to an exodus of experienced investigators and prosecutors.

item.title: How We Analyzed the Strike on the Iranian School
item.description: Malachy Browne of our Visual Investigations team describes what satellite imagery and other evidence tell us about who might be responsible for an airstrike on an elementary school in southern Iran. The strike killed at least 175 people, according to health officials and Iranian state media.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne, Aaron Boxerman, Coleman Lowndes and Estelle Caswell

item.title: Alan Trustman, Who Wrote ‘Bullitt’ and ‘Thomas Crown Affair,’ Dies at 95
item.description: In a wide-ranging career, he was a Boston lawyer, a Hollywood screenwriter and a Swiss currency trader.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: Trump Demands ‘Unconditional Surrender’ From Iran
item.description: Also, a weak jobs report. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Whet Moser

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Explosions at sunset west of Tehran.

item.title: Russia Is Sharing Intelligence With Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The information has included satellite imagery showing the locations of military personnel. But some officials played down the significance of the partnership.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Pavel Bednyakov
media:content.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia has spoken to his Iranian counterpart to discuss the U.S.-Israel attack.

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein Used Doctors to Control Women
item.description: Our investigative reporter David A. Fahrenthold describes how Jeffrey Epstein used a small circle of doctors to control the medical care of and manipulate the young women from overseas who were having sex with him.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold, Christina Shaman, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, June Kim and Whitney Shefte

item.title: Boston Symphony Abruptly Ends Andris Nelsons’ Contract
item.description: The orchestra’s leadership announced on Friday that it and the conductor Andris Nelsons “were not aligned on future vision.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney

item.title: Talarico’s Social Media Paper Trail, the Jobs Report and More Politics News
item.description: For some Democrats, generational change comes with a cringey social media past.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Talarico, the state representative from Texas who is now the Democratic nominee for Senate in the state.

item.title: Divisive F.D.A. Vaccine Regulator Is Resigning
item.description: Dr. Vinay Prasad drew criticism for overriding career scientists and rejecting drugs by companies seeking agency approval.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Food and Drug Administration
media:content.media:description: Dr. Vinay Prasad’s refusal to accept Moderna’s application for a new mRNA flu vaccine, prompting an uproar among companies and experts.

item.title: Here Is Mexico’s Plan to Keep the Country Safe During the World Cup
item.description: Officials announced a massive deployment of security forces ahead of the world’s largest sporting event after recent violence prompted safety concerns for the millions of fans expected to attend.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and David Shortell

media:content.media:credit: Raquel Cunha/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Banorte Stadium, also known as Azteca Stadium, the venue in Mexico City that will host the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: I’ve Never Been a Patriot, but Spain Standing Up to Trump Has Made Me One
item.description: There are times when it is clear what it means to stand up for what is right.
item.dc:creator: Paco Cerdà
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Jon Nazca/Reuters

item.title: Florida Bar Retreats From Statement Saying Lindsey Halligan Was Under Scrutiny
item.description: The Florida bar said that it had “erroneously” made that assertion, disclosed in a letter last month, and that no investigation into Ms. Halligan was pending.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Jacquelyn Martin/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A spokeswoman for the bar declined to comment on whether she had been contacted by administration officials.

item.title: Going to War With Iran, Surrounded by Yes Men
item.description: Members of President Trump’s cabinet see their jobs as “total obedience to the president’s will,” the columnist Jamelle Bouie argues, and that means we should all be very worried about how the war in Iran could unfold.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie and Ingrid Holmquist
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Pakistani Man Is Found Guilty of Plot to Kill Trump Backed by Iran
item.description: A federal jury in Brooklyn convicted Asif Merchant on Friday of planning to kill American politicians, in a scheme backed by the Iranian government. Mr. Merchant faces life in prison.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Asif Merchant was convicted of plotting to kill President Trump and other politicians in a plot backed by Iran on Friday.

item.title: Timothée Chalamet Has a Point About Ballet
item.description: This art form is alive, and Chalamet, who comes from a dance family, knows it. But what value does ballet have for the world at large?
item.dc:creator: Gia Kourlas

media:content.media:credit: James Devaney/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Timothée Chalamet at a Knicks game in 2019. He said this week, “I don’t want to be working in ballet or opera, or, you know, things where it’s like, ‘Hey, keep this thing alive.’”

item.title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
item.description: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Explosions over west Tehran on Friday evening.

item.title: A Quiet Winter Retreat in the French Riviera
item.description: The French Riviera is usually considered a busy summer hotspot with throngs of tourists, but during the winter months, this stretch of the Mediterranean coastline becomes a quiet, relaxing retreat.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling and Gianni Cipriano

media:content.media:credit: Gianni Cipriano for The New York Times

item.title: Iran Attacks and Mideast Flights: How Safe Is Air Travel?
item.description: Drone and missile attacks have caused high anxiety across the region, but experts say the danger to commercial airliners is “fairly remote.”
item.dc:creator: Gabe Castro-Root
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kai Pfaffenbach/Reuters
media:content.media:description: An Emirates airliner evacuating foreigners from Dubai arrived in Frankfurt on Tuesday.

item.title: Learn to Dog-Sled in Snowy Northern Minnesota
item.description: A hotel on the edge of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area in Ely, Minn., teaches guests how to harness, mush, and manage teams of three to six dogs during their stay.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal, Daniel Fetherston and Ben Brewer

media:content.media:description: A dog team pulls guests on a day trip from Wintergreen Dogsled Lodge, on the edge of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area in Ely, Minn.

item.title: Israeli Strikes Intensify in Beirut as Thousands of Lebanese Flee
item.description: About 300,000 people in Lebanon have fled their homes since bombing from the Israeli military began, the Norwegian Refugee Council estimated. ​​The Israeli military said it was targeting infrastructure belonging to Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper, Daniel Fetherston and Nader Ibrahim

media:content.media:credit: Ibrahim Amro/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Lebanese soldiers near a damaged hotel that was hit by an Israeli airstrike in Hazmieh, east of Beirut, on Wednesday.

item.title: Bob Power on His Work on A Tribe Called Quest’s ‘The Low End Theory’
item.description: A Tribe Called Quest had the vision for “The Low End Theory.” The engineer Bob Power helped piece it together.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Ord/WireImage for The Recording Academy, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Bob Power’s work on A Tribe Called Quest’s “The Low End Theory” has been lauded by listeners and critics since its release 35 years ago.

item.title: 48 Hours in the Life of Courrèges’s Nicolas Di Felice
item.description: In the lead-up to Courrèges fall fashion show, Nicolas Di Felice held fittings, oversaw rehearsals and gave notes on a floor meant to look like a city street.
item.dc:creator: Zoey Poll

item.title: Both Top Choices to Lead N.Y. Child Services Agency Are Off the List
item.description: The Mamdani administration had signaled it was open to making significant changes at the agency, and it was unclear on Friday what direction it will now take.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth A. Harris

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Mamdani administration had signaled that it was open to making significant changes at the A.C.S.

item.title: New Airstrikes Batter Tehran
item.description: The Israeli military said it had sent a broad wave of strikes at the Iranian capital early Saturday. Some strikes hit an area near the Mehrabad Airport. Footage shared on state media showed multiple explosions and columns of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
